name: kitty
description: Linux kitty terminal tools for Codex, using numbered managed and adopted kitty remote-control sockets for terminal listing, unified input, and screen reads.

interface.shortDescription: Send input to and inspect numbered kitty terminals
interface.longDescription: Kitty gives Codex Linux-native terminal tools for using visible numbered kitty terminals such as K1. The model-facing interface exposes kitty_list for available terminals and windows, kitty_send for shell commands, raw text, and key input with screen feedback, and kitty_read for the current terminal screen. Missing K-number targets are created as managed kitty instances, while future user-opened kitty launches can still be adopted through the user-level wrapper. It uses kitty's remote-control socket interface and does not change the user's global kitty configuration, use TCP sockets, retroactively adopt old terminals without sockets, or inject hidden status commands into the visible terminal.
